meta_title: Roofing in Highland Beach, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Expert roofing in Highland Beach, FL. Hurricane-rated CertainTeed & West Lake systems for luxury coastal homes. Licensed, insured, A+ BBB. Free estimates!
h1: Highland Beach's Premier Roofing Experts for Luxury Coastal Homes
intro:
Highland Beach is one of South Florida's most exclusive coastal communities — a narrow barrier island tucked between the Atlantic Ocean and the Intracoastal Waterway, where property values average $1.1 million and the standard of living demands nothing short of the best. From the grand residences of Toscana and Boca Highland Beach Club to the elegant condominiums of Dalton Place, homeowners here have invested significantly in properties that deserve equally significant protection. And in a community sitting just 0.3 miles from the Atlantic coast, there is no component of your home more critical to that protection than your roof.

Highland Beach sits in a 170 mph wind zone, making it one of the most demanding environments for roofing systems in all of Palm Beach County. While the area is not within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) that governs Miami-Dade and Broward, Florida Building Code still mandates hurricane-rated roofing products for all new and replacement installations — a requirement that's non-negotiable this close to open water. With approximately 50% of homes in Highland Beach built before 1994, and therefore before modern hurricane-resistant building codes were enacted, a substantial portion of local residences are long overdue for a full roof evaluation and potential replacement.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — known throughout South Florida as The Kings of Service — we understand the unique demands of coastal luxury homeowners in Highland Beach. We don't sell one-size-fits-all solutions. We assess each property individually, consider the architectural style, the exposure to salt air and wind-driven rain, and the long-term value you're working to protect. Whether your home overlooks the Intracoastal from Boca Highland Beach Club or sits steps from the sand, our roofing specialists deliver craftsmanship and materials worthy of the address.

section_why.h2: Why Highland Beach Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Every Roofing Project
section_why.body:
When you own a home worth over a million dollars in one of Palm Beach County's most coveted zip codes, you simply cannot afford to gamble on a roofing contractor who subcontracts the work, cuts corners on materials, or disappears after the final invoice. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing was built on a different philosophy — one that centers on accountability, craftsmanship, and long-term relationships with the homeowners we serve. With 60+ years of combined industry experience across our team and more than 500 homes successfully completed throughout Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, we've earned the trust of discerning homeowners from Gulf Stream to Boca Raton.

One of the most important distinctions between Premier Impact Windows & Roofing and other roofing companies serving the Highland Beach area is our 100% in-house installation model. Every crew member who shows up at your property is a trained, vetted Premier Impact Windows & Roofing employee — not a day laborer hired through a subcontractor network. This means consistent quality control, clear communication, and a single point of accountability from the moment we pull your permit with the Town of Highland Beach Building Department to the day we do our final walk-through inspection with you.

Our A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau isn't just a badge — it reflects years of doing right by homeowners exactly like you. We approach each roofing project in Highland Beach with the same level of detail: a thorough inspection of your existing deck, underlayment, flashing, and ventilation systems; a transparent proposal with no hidden fees; and a realistic timeline communicated upfront. We also coordinate directly with your insurance adjuster if storm damage is involved, helping you navigate claims and documentation so you can focus on what matters most.

We are fully licensed and insured to operate in Palm Beach County, and our project managers stay current on the latest Florida Building Code updates so your new roof is installed to the highest possible standard — not just what's minimally required. For the residents of Highland Beach, anything less simply isn't acceptable.

section_history.h2: A Proven Storm Threat: What Highland Beach Has Already Weathered
section_history.body:
Highland Beach has experienced no fewer than seven named storms since 1990, and each one has left a mark on the community's roofing landscape. The most significant of these was Hurricane Andrew in 1992 — a Category 5 storm that fundamentally reshaped Florida's building codes and exposed the catastrophic vulnerability of pre-modern roofing systems throughout South Florida. Many of the homes damaged or destroyed in the broader Palm Beach and Broward regions during Andrew had roofs that were never designed to withstand wind loads now considered standard. The aftermath of Andrew directly led to the sweeping code reforms that redefined what a hurricane-rated roof means in Florida.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 delivered another painful lesson to coastal communities along the Palm Beach County shoreline. With sustained winds and erratic pressure changes, Wilma caused widespread shingle blow-off, tile displacement, and structural damage to roofs that had never been updated since their original installation. Communities like Highland Beach, with a dense concentration of pre-1994 homes, bore the brunt of that vulnerability. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which, while not as catastrophically destructive as Andrew, caused significant roof damage throughout the coastal communities of South Palm Beach County — exposing deteriorated underlayment, failing flashing, and compromised deck attachments that years of salt air and UV exposure had quietly weakened.

For Highland Beach residents, these aren't abstract historical events — they're case studies in what happens when a roof isn't built or maintained to modern hurricane standards. With Atlantic storm seasons growing more intense and unpredictable, the question isn't whether your roof will face a serious storm. It's whether your roof will be ready when it does.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Compliance and Permitting for Highland Beach Roofing Projects
section_code.body:
Roofing in Highland Beach is governed by the Florida Building Code (FBC), Seventh Edition, as adopted and enforced locally by the Town of Highland Beach Building Department. While Highland Beach falls outside the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — which applies specifically to Miami-Dade and Broward counties — the area's 170 mph design wind speed classification still mandates the use of hurricane-rated roofing products and approved installation methods. This is not an area where standard residential roofing practices from other states or lower-wind regions apply.

For shingle roofing, the FBC requires products that carry a Florida Product Approval (FPA) with wind resistance ratings appropriate for the local design wind speed. CertainTeed's Landmark series, for example, carries product approvals that meet these requirements. Tile roofing systems must demonstrate uplift resistance through approved attachment methods — typically mechanically fastened or foam-set tile over reinforced underlayment systems. All replacement roofs in Highland Beach require a permit from the Town of Highland Beach Building Department, and work must be inspected at key stages including deck, underlayment, and final inspection before the permit can be closed.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process on your behalf. Our team prepares and submits all required documentation, product approval numbers, and installation specifications to the Town of Highland Beach Building Department before a single shingle or tile is installed. We schedule and pass all required inspections, and we provide you with a closed permit at the end of the project — an important document for your insurance policy, future insurance inspections, and eventual resale of your property. Buyers and insurers in Palm Beach County increasingly scrutinize roof permits, and a properly permitted roof by a licensed contractor is a meaningful asset.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed and West Lake: Hurricane-Grade Roofing Systems Built for Highland Beach
section_products.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've carefully selected our roofing product lines to match the specific demands of South Florida's coastal environment. For homeowners in Highland Beach who prefer the clean, classic look of dimensional shingles, we install CertainTeed's Landmark series — one of the most trusted architectural shingle systems in the country. The Landmark series features a dual-layer construction with a fiberglass mat base, SureStart PLUS warranty protection, and algae resistance designed to hold up in humid, salt-rich coastal climates. More importantly, CertainTeed Landmark shingles carry Florida Product Approvals for high-wind applications, making them fully compliant with Palm Beach County's 170 mph wind zone requirements. Their Class A fire rating and Class 4 impact resistance rating offer additional layers of protection that matter in a community this close to the Atlantic.

For homeowners in Toscana, Boca Highland Beach Club, or throughout Highland Beach who want the timeless Mediterranean aesthetic of tile roofing, we install West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. West Lake is a leading manufacturer of high-performance roofing tile with a long track record in Florida's coastal markets. Their concrete tile systems are engineered for superior wind uplift resistance, and their clay tile products offer the added benefit of natural thermal mass — helping to moderate attic temperatures and reduce cooling loads in South Florida's relentless summer heat. Both West Lake concrete and clay tile products are available in a wide range of profiles and colors to complement Highland Beach's luxury architectural styles.

Every Premier Impact Windows & Roofing roofing installation begins with a properly prepared substrate: we inspect and replace any damaged decking, install code-compliant underlayment systems, and apply precision flashing at all valleys, penetrations, and eave edges. The products we install are only as good as the system they're part of, and our in-house crews are trained specifically on CertainTeed and West Lake installation specifications to ensure every project performs as intended — storm after storm.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Highland Beach — and How Do You Finance It?
section_cost.body:
In a community where the average home value sits at $1.1 million, a new roof isn't an expense — it's a strategic investment in the long-term performance and value of a major asset. Roofing costs in Highland Beach vary based on the size and pitch of the roof, the chosen material system, the condition of the existing deck, and whether any structural repairs are needed during the replacement. As a general benchmark, architectural shingle replacements in the Highland Beach area typically range from $18,000 to $35,000 for standard-sized luxury homes, while tile roof replacements — particularly clay tile — can range from $30,000 to $70,000 or more depending on scope and complexity. These figures reflect code-compliant, fully permitted work by licensed professionals — not the cut-rate bids sometimes offered by unlicensed operators.

The return on that investment is substantial. A new hurricane-rated roof in Palm Beach County almost always generates significant wind mitigation insurance credits that reduce annual premiums — often by $2,000 to $5,000 or more per year for coastal properties. Over the 25- to 50-year lifespan of a quality roofing system, those savings accumulate into tens of thousands of dollars. Additionally, a new roof dramatically improves a home's appraisal value and marketability — critical in Highland Beach's competitive luxury real estate market, where buyers and their inspectors scrutinize roof condition and documentation carefully.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options to make roofing projects accessible without delay. Our programs start at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. We believe no Highland Beach homeowner should have to wait to protect a million-dollar home because of upfront cost concerns — especially with storm season as a constant reality. Our team will walk you through financing options alongside your project proposal so you can make the decision that works best for your financial situation.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings and Wind Mitigation Benefits for Highland Beach Roofing Upgrades
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling financial arguments for replacing an aging roof in Highland Beach is the direct impact it has on your homeowner's insurance premiums. Florida's property insurance market is among the most expensive in the nation, and Palm Beach County coastal properties — especially those in the 170 mph wind zone — carry some of the highest rates in the state. However, Florida law requires insurers to provide premium credits for homes that demonstrate verified wind mitigation features, and a new hurricane-rated roof is the single most impactful upgrade you can make to qualify for those credits.

After a Premier Impact Windows & Roofing roofing installation, we strongly recommend scheduling a wind mitigation inspection with a licensed inspector. The resulting Wind Mitigation Report documents your roof's deck attachment, roof covering type, roof deck material, and roof-to-wall connection — all factors that insurers use to calculate premium discounts. Homes in Highland Beach with newer roofs featuring reinforced underlayment, secondary water barriers, and approved high-wind shingle or tile products frequently qualify for substantial discounts with carriers serving Palm Beach County, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, and Heritage Insurance. These discounts can amount to 20% to 40% or more off the wind portion of your premium.

Beyond the ongoing premium savings, a properly documented roof replacement also strengthens your position when filing a claim after storm damage. Insurers are far more likely to pay claims quickly and in full when there is a clear record of a permitted, inspected, code-compliant roof installed by a licensed contractor. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides full documentation packages — including permit records, product approval numbers, and installation photos — that support both your wind mitigation inspection and any future claims process. In Highland Beach's high-value real estate environment, that documentation is worth its weight in gold.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Highland Beach home needs a full roof replacement or just repairs?
faqs.a: The best way to determine this is with a professional roof inspection. In Highland Beach, where approximately 50% of homes were built before 1994, many roofs are well past their intended service life. If your roof is over 20 years old, shows signs of widespread granule loss, cracked or displaced tiles, or has a history of repeated repairs, a full replacement is almost always the more cost-effective long-term solution. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers thorough inspections to assess your roof's true condition and give you an honest recommendation — not just the option that generates the most revenue.

faqs.q: What does a roof replacement cost in Highland Beach, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Architectural shingle replacements in Highland Beach typically range from $18,000 to $35,000, while tile systems can range from $30,000 to $70,000 depending on size, material, and complexity. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified homeowners, making it possible to protect your investment without a large upfront payment. Every proposal includes a transparent, itemized breakdown so you know exactly what you're paying for.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit for a roof replacement in Highland Beach, and how does that process work?
faqs.a: Yes, all roof replacements in Highland Beach require a permit from the Town of Highland Beach Building Department. The permit process involves submitting product approval documentation, installation specifications, and contractor licensing information. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles all of this on your behalf — from application through final inspection. You'll receive a closed permit at the end of your project, which is critical for insurance wind mitigation inspections, property resale, and compliance documentation.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take for a home in Highland Beach?
faqs.a: For most single-family homes in Highland Beach, a full shingle roof replacement takes two to four days of active installation once materials are delivered and the permit is active. Tile roofs may take five to seven days or longer depending on scope. The total project timeline from contract signing to completed permit closure is typically two to four weeks, accounting for permitting, material lead times, and inspection scheduling. Our project manager keeps you informed at every stage so there are no surprises.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Highland Beach?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. Florida law requires insurers to provide wind mitigation credits for homes with documented hurricane-resistant roofing features. A new CertainTeed or West Lake roofing system installed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing qualifies for a post-installation wind mitigation inspection, and the resulting report frequently earns Highland Beach homeowners 20% to 40% in premium reductions on the wind portion of their policy. Many homeowners find that the annual insurance savings alone offset a meaningful portion of the roof replacement cost over time.

cta_headline: Protect Your Highland Beach Home with a Roof Built to Last
cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free roofing inspection and estimate — because your Highland Beach home deserves The Kings of Service protecting it from the next storm season.
